architecture.summary: SCI-Verifier is a reasoning-augmented LLM-based verifier for scientific answer equivalence judgment. It takes a question, reference answer, and model-generated response as input and produces a binary correctness label (true/false) preceded by concise chain-of-thought reasoning. Information flows through a two-stage post-training pipeline: (1) supervised fine-tuning transfers short, curated reasoning traces from larger models into a smaller base model (Qwen3-4B or 8B), and (2) reinforcement learning (DAPO) refines reasoning quality while penalizing verbosity. The model outputs structured reasoning followed by a final judgment, designed to be both accurate and computationally efficient.

claims.statement: On equivalence-based answers from SCI-VerifyBench, SCI-Verifier-8B achieves substantially higher performance than GPT-5, particularly in mathematics and physics where GPT-5 scores drop below 50%
Chain-of-thought reasoning brings substantial gains in scientific verification across models of different scales

SCI-Verifier exhibits strong robustness to prompt modifications, maintaining competitive performance even when the prompt differs from those seen during training, whereas general models are considerably more sensitive to prompt variations
Combining supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning leads to consistently superior performance and better generalization across different datasets compared to SFT or RL alone

SCI-VerifyBench covers five disciplines (mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology, general QA) with 2,500 total samples (500 per domain) including 350 real LLM responses and 150 equivalence-based synthetic responses per domain, constructed with domain-specific equivalence transformations and hybrid LLM/expert annotations
